channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Town businessman faces court over alleged R4 million bribe to City Manager
item.description: Shaun Roos, a 56-year-old businessman from Cape Town, has appeared in court accused of attempting to bribe the City Manager, Lungelo Mbandazayo, with R4 million. The alleged bribe was intended to rein...

item.title: Five Nigerian Governors Join APC, Party Now Controls 31 States
item.description: For Nigeria, political matter don hot as five governors don recently waka join the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC). Dis movement dey show say APC dey try secure power ahead of the 2027 election...

item.title: Kim Chiu Shares Emotions Ahead of ‘The Alibi’ Primetime Premiere
item.description: Filipina actress Kim Chiu expressed heartfelt emotions as she prepares for the primetime premiere of her latest project, 'The Alibi', scheduled for March 16. The actress, known for her dedication and ...

item.title: British Forces Intercept Iranian Drones at Iraqi Base Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: British forces stationed in Iraq successfully intercepted and shot down two Iranian drones during overnight attacks on the coalition base in Erbil, the capital of the Kurdistan region, according to De...

item.title: Teenager dies in Melbourne car crash during stolen vehicle incident
item.description: A teenage boy has tragically died in a car crash in Melbourne while driving a stolen Skoda. The incident occurred earlier today, shocking the local community. Authorities are investigating the circums...

item.title: NiMet warns Northern Nigerians on increased risk of meningitis during hot season
item.description: NiMet don raise alarm say people wey dey some northern states for Nigeria fit dey more at risk of Cerebrospinal Meningitis (CSM) as weather don favour the spread of the sickness. For a nationwide advi...

item.title: Kenya to Hold Memorial for Veteran Rescuer Samuel Wangila
item.description: A memorial service for Samuel Wanyonyi Wangila, a veteran aqua rescuer, is scheduled for Thursday at All Saints Cathedral in Nairobi. Wangila tragically died on March 3 while trying to rescue a boy wh...

item.title: What Running Marathons Teaches About Leadership and Teamwork
item.description: Herman Kasekende reflects on how running long-distance races, like marathons and ultramarathons, can offer important lessons for leadership and managing teams. He recalls his first Nairobi Marathon in...

item.title: US military not yet prepared to escort oil tankers amid tensions in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has stated that it is not yet prepared to provide escort services to oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, as the US Navy's resources are currently prioritised for operations aga...

item.title: Estee Lauder sues Jo Malone over use of her name in new fragrance products
item.description: Jo Malone, the British fragrance entrepreneur who founded the self-named perfume brand Jo Malone, is facing legal action from cosmetics giant Estee Lauder Companies. After selling her original brand i...

item.title: Teacher suspended after mocking Grade 1 pupil over fundraiser pie
item.description: A teacher from Thembalethu Primary School in George has been temporarily suspended following a video that went viral, showing her mocking a Grade 1 learner for not being able to afford a pie during a ...

item.title: Sabalenka and Zverev advance to Indian Wells semi-finals after strong performances
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka and Alexander Zverev have both secured spots in the semi-finals of the Indian Wells tennis tournament following convincing wins on Thursday. Sabalenka, the world No. 1, faced Canadian ...

item.title: King Charles III Welcomes Prominent Nigerians in UK at St James’s Palace Reception Before Tinubu’s State Visit
item.description: King Charles III organise one big reception for Nigerian diaspora people for UK inside St James’s Palace on Monday. This one happen just before President Bola Tinubu go come do him state visit to Unit...

item.title: Legal Penalties for Defacing Kenyan Currency Notes
item.description: In Kenya, wilfully defacing, tearing, cutting or otherwise mutilating any currency note is a criminal offence under Section 367A of the Penal Code. Offenders face up to three months in prison, a fine ...

item.title: Banking app glitch briefly exposed other customers' transactions
item.description: Customers of Lloyds Bank, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland experienced a major privacy glitch when their banking apps mistakenly displayed the transactions of other users. The issue, which occurred on Th...

item.title: CHED and Landbank Launch ATM System to Streamline Scholarship Payouts for 2 Million Students
item.description: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) and Land Bank of the Philippines have partnered to improve the distribution of government scholarships and subsidies by providing ATM cards directly to studen...

item.title: Audit reveals millions spent on failed or delayed borehole projects in Kenyan counties
item.description: The article reports on how millions of shillings earmarked for borehole projects across several Kenyan counties appear to have been lost through failed or delayed drilling exercises. According to an a...

item.title: Christine Bergeron: Pioneering Vancouver Business Leader in Sustainable Finance and Community Development
item.description: Christine Bergeron, a visionary Vancouver business leader, passed away at 51 from glioblastoma. Fresh from an MBA in strategic management, she devoted her career to using finance for positive social a...

item.title: Eldoret court jails Jackson Kibor’s son for assault in inheritance row
item.description: A court in Uasin Gishu County has sentenced Jackson Kibor’s son to three years in jail after he was found guilty of assaulting his stepmother’s sister during a heated argument over family inheritance ...

item.title: Deadloch season two filmed in Northern Territory with new characters, crocs and comedy
item.description: The Emmy-nominated Australian crime comedy series Deadloch is back for its second season, relocating from Tasmania to the Northern Territory. Co-creators Kate McCartney and Kate McLennan discovered a ...
